guardrail_evaluations.explanation: There is no list price or discount in this deal — it is a rev-share partnership with no subscription fee. The discount guardrail does not meaningfully apply, but the deal has already been routed to RevOps, satisfying the intent of this rule.

No priced contract exists, so there is no discount to evaluate. This guardrail is structurally inapplicable to a pure rev-share model; the soft ceiling is technically met but not meaningful in this context.

There is no guaranteed subscription revenue and no baseline fee — Clay's margin is entirely contingent on Reverberate's downstream sales performance. In a worst-case zero-revenue scenario, Clay incurs infrastructure and support costs with no corresponding income, violating the 25% floor. The deal must be restructured to include a minimum floor payment or baseline fee to satisfy this guardrail before approval.

Even using the midpoint rev-share estimate (~$800K/yr at 25% = $200K Clay contribution), the effective margin depends entirely on Reverberate's client billings — there is no structural guarantee. This rule flags until a minimum committed baseline is established.

alternative_structures.label: Baseline Subscription + Reduced Rev Share
alternative_structures.description: Require Reverberate to pay a guaranteed annual platform access fee (e.g., $60K/yr as a white-label partner tier) with a reduced rev-share rate of 20% on Reverberate Engine subscriptions. Clay gets predictable margin-positive revenue regardless of downstream client volumes.
alternative_structures.rationale: The baseline fee anchors Clay's economics against a zero-revenue downside scenario and satisfies the gross margin floor guardrail. Reverberate still benefits from white-label rights and co-marketing; Clay trades 5 rev-share points for structural protection. Margin estimate assumes the $60K fee approximates a standard self-serve contract priced at list, maintaining the 40% gross margin working assumption.

alternative_structures.label: Tiered Rev Share with Minimum Annual Commit
alternative_structures.description: Structure the rev share as a minimum annual commit (MAC) of $120K/yr remitted to Clay (i.e., guaranteed floor regardless of Reverberate Engine performance), with the 25% rev share applying above that floor. If Reverberate Engine generates more than $480K in client billings, the 25% share kicks in and effectively acts as a true-up. Early access and co-marketing rights are retained.
alternative_structures.rationale: A MAC converts the contingent rev-share into a partially guaranteed revenue stream, clearing the margin floor guardrail. Reverberate is protected in downside scenarios (the MAC is likely achievable even at partial ramp) while Clay captures upside through the 25% share on volumes above the floor. This structure also sets a clear anchor for renewal escalators.

alternative_structures.label: Co-Sell Partnership with Referral Credit Model (No White-Label)
alternative_structures.description: Replace white-labeling with a co-sell or referral model: Reverberate recommends and implements Clay directly for their clients under their own brand consulting, earning a 15% referral credit on net-new ACV they source. Clay retains full brand presence, full subscription margin, and avoids the IP/branding risk of white-labeling. Early access and co-marketing rights are retained as relationship incentives.
alternative_structures.rationale: White-labeling creates brand dilution and long-term channel dependency risk. A referral/co-sell model converts Reverberate into a demand-gen and implementation partner instead, maintaining Clay's direct client relationships and full-margin subscription revenue. Using Reverberate's 8 top-50 client relationships as a referral pipeline (estimated $200K net-new ACV at 15% referral credit = $30K cost) is structurally cleaner for Clay's GTM. Margin estimate reflects 15% referral discount applied against the 40% gross margin baseline assumption.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal has no list price, no proposed price, and no subscription fee — it is a pure contingent rev-share partnership, which sits entirely outside the standard pricing guardrail framework. The gross margin absolute floor (block_absolute) technically fires because there is no guaranteed revenue baseline, meaning Clay's margin in a downside scenario is zero or negative. All discount-based guardrails are inapplicable but pass by default at 0%. The 40% gross margin working assumption (per system instructions) cannot be applied meaningfully here since COGS are real but revenue is contingent — the CFO and partnerships team should model cost-to-serve for white-label infrastructure separately before approving. Confidence is low due to the non-standard pricing model, absence of any committed revenue, and the fact that none of the precedent deals are structurally analogous to a rev-share partnership.

performance_obligations.name: White-label license of Clay tables and waterfalls (Reverberate Engine)
performance_obligations.description: Grant of right to brand and resell Clay's table/waterfall functionality under Reverberate's product name to Reverberate's clients over the 24-month term.
performance_obligations.rationale: The license is functional IP delivered as access; revenue is a sales-based royalty (25% rev share) and under ASC 606-10-55-65 (royalty exception) is recognized as the underlying subscriptions occur.

performance_obligations.name: Co-marketing rights and client-attributed case studies
performance_obligations.description: Right for Reverberate to use Clay branding/case-study collateral and reference their clients in joint marketing.
performance_obligations.rationale: Co-marketing is likely not separately identifiable from the white-label partnership and provides no standalone value to Reverberate apart from the license; treated as part of the combined partnership PO.

performance_obligations.name: Early access to new Clay features (30-day advance)
performance_obligations.description: Reverberate receives Clay product features 30 days before general availability throughout the term.
performance_obligations.rationale: A stand-ready obligation to provide pre-GA access continuously over the term; capable of being distinct because it confers value independent of the white-label license.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: Consideration is entirely a 25% sales-based royalty on Reverberate Engine subscriptions. Under ASC 606-10-55-65, recognize at the later of when the underlying sale occurs or the related PO is satisfied. Range $400K–$1.2M/yr makes any TCV estimate highly uncertain.

If Clay provides marketing support or client attribution that benefits Reverberate, evaluate under ASC 606-10-32-25 whether any value transferred constitutes consideration payable to a customer that should reduce the transaction price.

Stand-ready early-access obligation has no observable SSP; allocation from a fully variable royalty stream is inherently difficult and may require an estimated SSP using cost-plus or adjusted-market approaches.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: Because there is no fixed fee and the entire economics are a 25% royalty, any future change to the rev-share rate, scope of features white-labeled, or exclusivity terms would constitute a contract modification under ASC 606-10-25-10. Additionally, the 30-day early access could be modified if Clay changes its release cadence, potentially reopening the contract.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Midpoint estimate of $400K–$1.2M annual rev-share; recognized as underlying Reverberate Engine subscription sales occur (royalty exception).
Midpoint estimate; subject to actual underlying subscription volume.

red_flags.label: No fixed consideration — pure rev share
red_flags.explanation: Deal has $0 ACV/TCV with all economics tied to a 25% sales-based royalty. Requires CFO and partnership-accounting policy review before signing; standard approval matrix does not apply.

red_flags.label: White-label IP licensing precedent
red_flags.explanation: This is Clay's first white-label arrangement of core functionality. Sets accounting and legal precedent for IP licensing (functional vs. symbolic IP determination under ASC 606-10-55-58) that will affect future partnership deals.

red_flags.label: Channel conflict / consideration payable to customer
red_flags.explanation: 8 of Reverberate's clients are also Clay top-50 customers. Risk that rev-share payments effectively reduce direct subscription revenue from those customers, potentially requiring reclassification as consideration payable to a customer (ASC 606-10-32-25).

red_flags.label: SSP allocation difficulty
red_flags.explanation: Allocating transaction price between the white-label license, early-access obligation, and co-marketing is difficult absent observable standalone prices, especially with fully variable consideration.

red_flags.label: Quarterly net-45 remittance
red_flags.explanation: Reliance on Reverberate's self-reported subscription revenue creates audit/collectibility considerations; ensure audit rights and reporting controls are contractually specified.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a non-standard partnership with zero fixed fee and entirely variable, sales-based royalty consideration, qualifying for the ASC 606-10-55-65 royalty exception with revenue recognized as Reverberate's underlying subscriptions occur. Multiple distinct performance obligations (white-label license, early access, possibly co-marketing) require SSP allocation that is highly judgmental given the variable consideration and lack of comparable transactions. Channel-conflict and consideration-payable-to-customer risks compound the modification risk inherent in a royalty-only structure. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes to white-label Clay's tables and waterfall functionality under their own branded product 'Reverberate Engine,' presented to their clients without Clay attribution.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Unrestricted white-labeling severs Clay's brand visibility from its own product surface across 50+ mid-market clients, eliminating organic PLG conversion and word-of-mouth in the GTM ops community. If Reverberate churns or the partnership ends, Clay has no direct relationship with end-clients and no contractual path to convert them. White-label rights without scope constraints (field-of-use, geography, client count cap) are also typically non-revocable in practice even with termination clauses, because clients have already been sold 'Reverberate Engine' as a product.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: White-label rights are granted solely for Reverberate's direct client engagements in the GTM agency services vertical, limited to a maximum of [75] named end-client accounts per contract year (expandable by written amendment). Clay retains the right to require 'Powered by Clay' attribution in product UI footers and in all Reverberate Engine end-user documentation. White-label rights are non-transferable, non-sublicensable, and terminate automatically upon expiration or early termination of this Agreement. Reverberate shall provide Clay a current list of all end-clients using the white-labeled product within 15 days of each quarter-end.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept white-label without mandatory UI attribution if Reverberate agrees to: (a) a hard cap of 100 named end-client accounts, (b) co-branded 'Reverberate Engine, powered by Clay' in sales collateral and onboarding materials (not necessarily in-product UI), and (c) Clay's right to contact end-clients directly upon termination of this Agreement for conversion offers.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has not previously executed a white-label agreement; this would be a novel contract type requiring VP-level and legal sign-off.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes a 25% revenue share on all Reverberate Engine subscription fees collected from their clients, remitted quarterly on net-45 terms, with no baseline subscription fee to Clay.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A 25% rev-share with no floor creates zero revenue certainty for Clay — if Reverberate underprices Reverberate Engine subscriptions, discounts aggressively, or shifts clients to a services-only model, Clay's receipts collapse without any contractual remedy. Net-45 from quarter-close is slow relative to Clay's standard billing cadence and creates reconciliation risk given Clay has no direct visibility into Reverberate's billing. No audit right means Clay cannot verify the rev-share base, and there is no minimum annual guarantee to underwrite the infrastructure and support cost of the partnership.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay shall receive the greater of: (a) twenty-five percent (25%) of Gross Revenue collected by Reverberate from Reverberate Engine subscriptions in each quarter, or (b) a Minimum Quarterly Guarantee of $[25,000] per quarter commencing Q3 2026 (increasing to $[35,000] from Q5 onward). 'Gross Revenue' means all fees invoiced to end-clients for Reverberate Engine access, excluding only sales taxes and documented refunds. Reverberate shall maintain accurate books and records and shall permit Clay (or a mutually agreed independent auditor) to audit such records no more than once per contract year upon 30 days' written notice. Remittance shall be accompanied by a detailed revenue report itemizing fees by end-client account.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept no minimum guarantee if Reverberate agrees to a quarterly revenue report with per-client detail and a unilateral audit right exercisable once per year; also accept net-60 remittance in exchange for 1.5% monthly late-payment interest after the due date.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has referral fee structures in place but no precedent for a rev-share partnership with audit rights; novel language required.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests co-marketing rights including the right to publish case studies featuring their clients, with client attribution visible in those materials.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Co-marketing rights that flow through Reverberate (not directly from Clay) mean Clay has no control over how its brand is represented in case studies, and Reverberate's clients have not consented to Clay's use of their names — only Reverberate's. If a case study mentions a mutual client's internal data or competitive stack, Clay could face downstream NDA or confidentiality claims from that client. The founder's 'vendor-agnostic' positioning (see playbook signal) suggests case studies may be crafted to highlight Reverberate's methodology over Clay's product, reducing Clay's marketing value from the arrangement.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: All co-marketing materials, including case studies, blog posts, and social content referencing Clay's brand, technology, or results attributable to Clay's platform, must be (a) approved in writing by Clay's marketing team prior to publication, with a 10-business-day review SLA, (b) accurately attribute Clay as the underlying platform where Clay's functionality is material to the described outcome, and (c) comply with Clay's then-current brand guidelines. Reverberate represents that it has obtained all necessary consents from referenced end-clients prior to submission for Clay's review. Either party may request removal of published materials naming that party within 30 days of a written request, which the publishing party shall honor within 15 days.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept Reverberate publishing case studies under their own brand without mandatory Clay attribution, provided Clay retains veto rights over any materials that (a) use Clay's name or logo, or (b) make performance claims attributable to Clay's platform.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay's standard partner agreements include co-marketing approval rights; this counter aligns with existing partner playbook language.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests access to new Clay features 30 days before public launch.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Blanket 30-day early access across all features creates an obligation Clay cannot always honor — certain releases (security patches, infrastructure migrations, regulated-data features) cannot safely be staged to a third party. If Reverberate white-labels an early-access feature that is later changed or pulled before GA, their end-clients receive a broken or deprecated experience under the 'Reverberate Engine' brand, creating support and reputational liability for Clay. Unrestricted early access also gives Reverberate's hired partnerships lead — explicitly building competitive intelligence on vendor infra — a window into Clay's roadmap that could inform competitive positioning.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay will use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Reverberate with beta access to new Reverberate Engine-relevant features no fewer than 14 days prior to general availability ('GA'), subject to: (a) execution of Clay's standard Beta Participation Agreement, including confidentiality and no-reverse-engineering obligations; (b) Clay's sole discretion to exclude features that are security-sensitive, infrastructure-level, or under regulatory review; and (c) Clay's right to accelerate or delay GA release timelines without liability. Early access features are provided 'as-is' with no SLA obligations until GA. Reverberate may not publicly disclose or incorporate pre-GA features into end-client-facing products without Clay's prior written consent.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept 30-day early access for product features directly used in the Reverberate Engine workflow, contingent on execution of the Beta Participation Agreement and the confidentiality/no-disclosure obligations above; retain the security/infra carve-out.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered beta access to select enterprise customers under Beta Participation Agreements; the 30-day window is longer than standard (typically 14 days) but not unprecedented for strategic partners.

redline.one_line_summary: We cannot ship this as-is — the white-label grant needs a named-account cap, 'Powered by Clay' attribution, and termination-linked wind-down rights, and the rev-share needs a minimum quarterly guarantee plus audit rights before legal will sign off.
redline.reasoning_summary: This is Clay's first white-label partnership deal, meaning every flagged clause involves novel contract territory without established internal precedent — the `block` priority is driven by the combination of an uncapped white-label grant (which could permanently sever Clay's brand from 50+ mid-market accounts) and a rev-share structure with no floor and no audit right (creating both revenue uncertainty and financial opacity). The founder's publicly stated 'vendor-agnostic' philosophy and the hire of a dedicated partnerships lead signal that Reverberate is sophisticated and has likely templated these terms to favor their own operating model. Clay has distribution leverage here — Reverberate's GTM value is real but so is their rebuild cost — so the recommended counters lean toward protecting Clay's brand and economics without killing the deal.

required_approvers.rationale: Deal type is partnership; all partnership deals route to CEO per matrix default rule.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: White-label and rev-share clauses require Legal review
required_approvers.rationale: Deal contains multiple high-risk non-standard clauses (white_label_branding, rev_share_25pct) flagged by Redline Agent as block priority; Legal must negotiate scope caps, audit rights, and attribution before execution.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Revenue recognition and margin guardrail blockers require Finance/CFO
required_approvers.rationale: Pricing Agent flags gross margin absolute floor violation (block_absolute) due to zero guaranteed revenue; ASC 606 Agent flags red flags including zero fixed consideration and channel-conflict risk requiring CFO/accounting policy review.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Partnership / rev-share routes to CEO + Strategic Partnership Review
required_approvers.rationale: Non-standard partnership with white-label, rev-share, and co-marketing components involving 8 top-50 customer overlap requires structured strategic partnership governance.

blockers_to_address_first: Redline block: White-label grant must be scoped to named-account cap (e.g., 75–100 end-clients per contract year), include 'Powered by Clay' attribution in product UI and sales collateral, and specify automatic termination and wind-down rights upon agreement expiration. AE must obtain Reverberate's written agreement to these constraints before Legal review.

Redline block: Rev-share structure must include a Minimum Quarterly Guarantee (e.g., $25K–$35K/quarter) to satisfy Pricing Agent's gross margin absolute floor guardrail. Alternative: add a baseline annual subscription fee (e.g., $60K–$120K/yr) in addition to or in place of rev-share. Without one of these, Finance will block on margin risk.

Redline block: Rev-share payment terms must include Clay audit rights (unilateral audit once per contract year) and detailed quarterly revenue reporting by end-client account. Net-45 remittance is acceptable if Reverberate agrees to 1.5% monthly late-payment interest and quarterly reconciliation.

ASC 606 red flag (block_without_approval): Deal structure has $0 fixed ACV/TCV and no revenue certainty. CFO must pre-approve the accounting treatment (sales-based royalty exception under ASC 606-10-55-65) and confirm that rev-share revenue is recognizable as Reverberate's underlying subscriptions occur. This requires CFO + audit/accounting policy review before Legal finalizes terms.

ASC 606 red flag (warn): Channel-conflict risk — 8 of Reverberate's clients are also Clay top-50 customers. Finance must assess whether rev-share payments to Reverberate effectively reduce direct subscription revenue from those customers, potentially triggering reclassification as 'consideration payable to a customer' under ASC 606-10-32-25. If so, transaction-price allocation methodology must be pre-approved by CFO and external audit.

Pricing guardrail (block_absolute): Gross margin floor violated — no guaranteed revenue and all economics are contingent on Reverberate's billing performance. Deal must be restructured before Pricing Agent sign-off. Recommended alternatives: (1) Baseline Subscription + Reduced Rev Share (e.g., $60K/yr + 20% rev-share), (2) Tiered Rev Share with Minimum Annual Commit (e.g., $120K MAC floor + 25% above), or (3) Referral/Co-Sell Model (15% referral credit on net-new ACV instead of white-label). AE must confirm Reverberate's willingness to one of these models in writing.

Legal precedent: This is Clay's first white-label partnership involving core functionality IP. Legal must establish internal precedent for white-label IP licensing (functional vs. symbolic IP under ASC 606-10-55-58) and document carve-outs for security/infrastructure/regulated features. Standard Beta Participation Agreement with confidentiality and no-reverse-engineering obligations is required for early-access rights.

Co-marketing approval: All co-marketing materials (case studies, blog posts, social content) referencing Clay's brand, technology, or product results must be pre-approved by Clay's marketing team with 10-business-day review SLA, and must comply with Clay's brand guidelines. Reverberate must obtain end-client consent for use of names in case studies. AE should confirm Reverberate agrees to 'veto rights for any Clay-attributed performance claims' before submission.
approval.one_line_summary: Partnership deal routes to AE Manager → Legal + Finance (parallel, both can veto) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO (veto). Expect 12 business days. Block-level issues: white-label scope must be capped with 'Powered by Clay' attribution, rev-share must include Minimum Quarterly Guarantee (or alternative baseline fee) to clear gross margin floor guardrail, audit rights and quarterly revenue reporting required, and CFO must pre-approve ASC 606 royalty-exception accounting plus channel-conflict reclassification risk before Legal finalizes terms.
approval.reasoning_summary: The deal fires the matrix default rule for partnership deals (routes to CEO) and triggers secondary approval chains due to multiple block-level issues from upstream Pricing, ASC 606, and Redline agents. The Pricing Agent flags an absolute-floor guardrail violation (zero guaranteed revenue, all contingent rev-share), requiring restructuring before Finance sign-off. The Redline Agent's block priority stems from uncapped white-label rights and unsecured rev-share with no audit path — both novel contract territory for Clay. The ASC 606 Agent flags revenue-recognition uncertainty and channel-conflict risk requiring pre-approval by CFO/audit. Legal must negotiate Legal+Finance in parallel (step 2) given the overlapping financial and legal red flags, followed by Strategic Partnership Review (step 3) to assess distribution value and precedent risk, and finally CEO sign-off (step 4) as the ultimate partnership approver. AE has multiple structural blockers to resolve (MQG/baseline fee, white-label cap, audit rights, co-marketing approval, CFO accounting pre-approval) before submission. Confidence is medium because the deal is structurally sound in intent but requires material restructuring on financial and legal terms; the approval chain itself is straightforward once blockers are cleared.

text.text: Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership — Reverberate Growth

fields.text: *Margin:* Block — zero guaranteed revenue; margin floor violated
*Approval Chain:* AE Manager → Legal + Finance (parallel, both veto) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO (veto) · Est. 12 business days

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *Deal cannot move forward as structured.* The rev-share-only model (no baseline fee, no minimum guarantee) fires an absolute gross margin floor block. The deal must be restructured — either with a baseline platform fee ($60K–$120K/yr) or a Minimum Quarterly Guarantee ($25K–$35K/quarter) — before Finance and Pricing will clear it.

• *White-label grant is uncapped and legally unprecedented for Clay.* Granting unrestricted white-label rights across 50+ mid-market clients with no named-account cap, no "Powered by Clay" attribution, and no post-termination wind-down path severs Clay's brand from its own product surface at scale. Legal must negotiate scope caps and attribution requirements before execution; this is Clay's first-ever white-label partnership of core IP.

• *CFO and audit pre-approval required before Legal finalizes terms.* The entire economics are a sales-based royalty (ASC 606-10-55-65 exception); there is no fixed TCV to recognize. Channel-conflict risk is also live — 8 of Reverberate's clients are Clay top-50 customers, raising potential reclassification of rev-share payments as consideration payable to a customer. CFO must sign off on accounting treatment and channel-conflict exposure before the contract is finalized.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Devon Wright (Mgr: Sarah Goldstein)   |   review filed by Kiln · agent-driven   |   2026-05-05

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership — Reverberate Growth
Deal Type: Partnership (Rev-Share) | ACV: $0 contracted, est. $400K–$1.2M/yr at 25% rev share | Term: 24 months | Close: June 1, 2026
Approval chain: AE Manager → Legal + Finance (parallel, both veto) → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO — est. 12 business days.
Key blockers: (1) Zero guaranteed revenue violates gross margin floor — must add baseline fee or minimum quarterly guarantee before Finance clears. (2) White-label grant is uncapped with no attribution or wind-down rights — Legal must negotiate scope caps; this is Clay's first white-label IP deal. (3) CFO must pre-approve ASC 606 royalty-exception treatment and channel-conflict risk (8 shared top-50 clients) before Legal finalizes terms.
AE Owner: Devon Wright | Review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Reverberate] Deal-desk review complete — 3 blockers before you can submit for approval
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Devon — deal-desk has completed its review of the Reverberate Engine white-label partnership. **This deal cannot be submitted for approval in its current form.** Three structural blockers must be resolved first, and you'll need written confirmation from Reverberate on each before Legal will touch it.

1. **Add a revenue floor.** The pure 25% rev-share with no baseline fee violates our gross margin absolute floor (block_absolute). Get Reverberate to agree in writing to one of: (a) $60K/yr platform fee + 20% rev-share, (b) $120K minimum annual commit with 25% applying above the floor, or (c) a referral/co-sell model at 15% credit in lieu of white-label rights.
2. **Scope the white-label grant.** Uncapped white-label rights are a legal block. Counter with a named-account cap (75–100 end-clients/year), "Powered by Clay" attribution in product UI and sales collateral, and automatic wind-down rights on termination. Reverberate must agree before Legal drafts terms.
3. **Secure audit rights and quarterly revenue reporting.** Net-45 remittance with no audit path is unacceptable — rev-share payment must include per-client quarterly revenue reports and a unilateral annual audit right. Align on this before submission or Finance will veto.

Once Reverberate confirms all three in writing, submit to Sarah for Step 1 sign-off. Approval chain from there: **Sarah (Step 1) → Legal + Finance in parallel, both have veto (Step 2) → Strategic Partnership Review (Step 3) → CEO, veto (Step 4).** Budget 12 business days from clean submission — do not start the clock until the blockers above are resolved.

customer_email_draft.subject: Reverberate Engine Partnership — Clay's Position on Key Terms
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We've had a chance to review your partnership proposal in full and genuinely appreciate the structure you've put forward — the Reverberate Engine practice represents real distribution value, and we want to find terms that work for both sides over the 24-month term.

On white-label rights: we can support white-labeling Clay's tables and waterfall functionality for Reverberate Engine, but we need to scope it to a named-account cap (we're proposing 75 accounts per contract year, expandable by written amendment) and require 'Powered by Clay' attribution in product UI footers and end-user documentation — this is a condition of Legal sign-off and not a point we can move off entirely, though we have a fallback to co-branded sales collateral if in-product UI attribution is operationally difficult for you.

On rev share: the 25% rate is workable, but we cannot execute against a structure with zero revenue floor — our finance and margin guardrails require either a Minimum Quarterly Guarantee (we're modeling $25K–$35K/quarter, stepping up from Q5) or a baseline annual platform fee ($60K–$120K/yr) paired with a reduced rev-share rate around 20%; we're open to which structure fits your model better and can run both scenarios side by side.

The rev-share terms also need to include a quarterly revenue report itemized by end-client and a unilateral audit right (once per contract year, 30-day notice) — this is a Finance requirement given we have no direct billing visibility into Reverberate Engine subscriptions.

On early access: we can offer 14-day pre-GA access (rather than 30) for features directly relevant to Reverberate Engine workflows, subject to execution of our standard Beta Participation Agreement and a carve-out for security, infrastructure, and regulated-data releases — this protects your clients from exposure to unstable surfaces and protects Clay from roadmap disclosure risk.

On co-marketing: we support joint case studies and co-marketing, and we'd ask for a 10-business-day approval right on any materials referencing Clay's brand or attributing performance outcomes to Clay's platform; our standard partner playbook language covers this and shouldn't be a heavy lift.

Our internal approval chain (Legal, Finance, Strategic Partnerships, and CEO) has an estimated 12-business-day cycle once we have Reverberate's written agreement in principle on the white-label cap, minimum revenue floor structure, and audit rights — can we get 30 minutes this week to align on those three points so we can move the redlined order form forward in parallel?

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Reverberate Growth (GTM agency, #18 of top 50, 80 employees, $12M ARR est.) proposes a 24-month white-label partnership: Clay tables and waterfalls rebranded as "Reverberate Engine," distributed to their 50+ mid-market clients in exchange for a 25% rev share ($400K–$1.2M/yr estimated) with no baseline subscription fee, plus co-marketing rights and 30-day early feature access.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
**Structure:** Pure rev-share — $0 ACV, $0 TCV, no list price, no subscription fee.
**Effective discount:** N/A (not a priced contract).
**Revenue range:** $400K–$1.2M/yr (midpoint ~$800K); entirely contingent on Reverberate's downstream client billings.
**Margin status:** BLOCKED — gross margin absolute floor guardrail fires (block_absolute); zero guaranteed revenue means Clay could incur infrastructure/support costs with $0 receipts.

**Alternative structures proposed:**
1. **Baseline Subscription + Reduced Rev Share** — $60K/yr platform fee + 20% rev share; est. 40% margin, clears floor guardrail.
2. **Tiered Rev Share with Minimum Annual Commit (MAC)** — $120K/yr floor guarantee + 25% rev share above $480K in client billings; est. 40% margin.
3. **Co-Sell / Referral Model (no white-label)** — 15% referral credit on net-new ACV sourced by Reverberate (~$200K est. ACV); est. 34% margin, eliminates brand and IP risk entirely.

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**BLOCK — Redline (Legal):**
- White-label grant is uncapped: no named-account limit, no "Powered by Clay" attribution, no post-termination wind-down rights. Clay loses brand visibility across 50+ mid-market accounts with no contractual path to convert end-clients if partnership ends.
- Rev-share has no minimum floor and no audit rights. Clay cannot verify the rev-share base; Reverberate controls all billing data.

**BLOCK — Pricing (Finance):**
- Gross margin absolute floor violated (0% guaranteed vs. 25% required threshold). Deal must be restructured with a baseline fee or MAC before Finance sign-off.

**BLOCK — ASC 606 (CFO required):**
- $0 fixed ACV; all revenue is a sales-based royalty recognized under ASC 606-10-55-65 (royalty exception) as Reverberate's underlying subscriptions occur. CFO must pre-approve accounting treatment before Legal finalizes terms.
- 8 of Reverberate's clients are also Clay top-50 customers — rev-share payments may require reclassification as "consideration payable to a customer" (ASC 606-10-32-25), reducing Clay's recognized transaction price on those accounts.

**WARN — Legal / IP:**
- First-ever white-label of Clay core functionality; sets legal and accounting precedent (functional vs. symbolic IP under ASC 606-10-55-58) for all future partnership deals.
- Early-access grant (30 days) is uncapped — security patches, infra migrations, and regulated-data features cannot be safely staged; requires carve-out and Beta Participation Agreement with confidentiality and no-reverse-engineering obligations.
- Co-marketing rights give Reverberate unilateral publishing control over case studies referencing Clay's platform and mutual clients, with no Clay approval right or brand-guideline compliance requirement.

**INFO:**
- Quarterly net-45 remittance with no per-client revenue reporting; audit/collectibility risk without contractual reporting controls.
- Founder's publicly stated "vendor-agnostic" philosophy and recent hire of a dedicated partnerships lead signal Reverberate is sophisticated and likely templating terms to favor their operating model.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) | Must confirm Reverberate's written agreement to structural counters before submission |
| 2 (parallel) | Legal + Finance/CFO | Both carry veto. Legal negotiates white-label scope, audit rights, attribution. CFO pre-approves ASC 606 royalty treatment and channel-conflict reclassification risk. |
| 3 | Strategic Partnership Review | Assesses distribution value, precedent risk, and top-50 customer overlap |
| 4 | CEO | Final veto; required for all partnership deals per matrix default |

**Estimated cycle time:** 12 business days (post-blocker resolution).
**Target close:** 2026-06-01 — cycle is tight; blockers must be resolved immediately.

**Pre-submission blockers AE must clear first:**
- Reverberate written agreement to: (a) named-account cap (75–100 end-clients/yr), (b) "Powered by Clay" attribution in UI/collateral, (c) Minimum Quarterly Guarantee of $25K–$35K or alternative baseline fee, (d) quarterly per-client revenue reporting + annual audit right.
- CFO accounting pre-approval of royalty-exception treatment and channel-conflict assessment.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**HOLD — Ship with conditions.**

The strategic rationale is real (distribution leverage, rebuild-risk conversion, 8 top-50 client relationships), but the deal cannot be executed as proposed: the uncapped white-label grant permanently severs Clay's brand from 50+ accounts, the zero-floor rev-share creates margin and revenue-recognition blockers that Finance and CFO will not clear, and every major clause represents novel legal territory with no internal precedent.

Route back to AE to secure Reverberate's written agreement to a minimum quarterly guarantee (or $60K–$120K/yr baseline fee), a named-account cap with "Powered by Clay" attribution, and audit rights — then resubmit through the full 4-step chain.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to collaborative tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~12 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing sits entirely outside the guardrail framework with $0 committed revenue and a contingent rev-share that trips the absolute gross-margin floor, so Finance cannot sign off until a baseline fee or MQG is modeled. ASC 606 treatment relies on the sales-based royalty exception but carries five red flags, multiple distinct performance obligations needing SSP allocation, and channel-conflict exposure that put contract-modification risk on the table and require CFO/auditor pre-approval. Redline is block-priority: an uncapped white-label grant could permanently sever Clay's brand from 50+ mid-market accounts, and the rev-share has no floor and no audit right — both novel territory for Clay's first white-label partnership. Approval routes AE manager → Legal → Finance → Strategic Partnership Review → CEO over roughly 12 business days, and the recommended call is ship-with-conditions in a collaborative tone, contingent on clearing the baseline-fee, white-label cap, audit-rights, and accounting blockers before submission.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth crosses 50 active mid-market clients, formalizes 'Reverberate Engine' practice
signals.summary: GTM agency Reverberate Growth announced its 50th active client and the formal launch of 'Reverberate Engine,' a productized service line that bundles outbound and enrichment workflows for mid-market RevOps teams.

signals.headline: Reverberate founder publishes 2026 GTM Operator Playbook, 14k subscribers in two weeks
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth's founder released the '2026 GTM Operator Playbook,' a long-form essay on agency-led RevOps that gained 14,000 subscribers in its first two weeks. The post argues for vendor-agnostic agency partnerships over single-vendor lock-in.

signals.headline: Reverberate Growth named #18 on annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth ranked #18 on the annual 'Top 50 GTM Agencies' list (up from #34 the prior year), with judges citing strong client outcomes in enrichment-heavy outbound and waterfall enrichment design.

signals.headline: GTM agencies announce vendor partnerships at 2026 RevOps Summit
signals.summary: At the RevOps Summit, several top-50 GTM agencies — including Reverberate — were named in vendor co-launch sessions. Industry observers describe a shift toward agency-vendor revenue-share partnerships rather than referral fees.

signals.headline: Reverberate hires former PLG-vendor head of partnerships
signals.summary: Reverberate Growth hired a former head of partnerships from a major PLG vendor to lead its new 'Strategic Vendor Partnerships' practice — explicitly framed as a path to revenue-share deals with infrastructure providers.
